description_long: This object is a delicate flower with a spherical cluster of small, white petals arranged densely around a central green core. The petals are narrow, slightly pointed, and overlap each other in a radial pattern, creating a layered, almost globe-like appearance. The flower is supported by a long, slender, dark green stem that is mostly straight but slightly curved at the base. Subtle hints of green are visible at the base of the petals, suggesting the presence of sepals or young leaves. The overall structure is lightweight and airy, typical of wildflowers found in urban parks or roadside verges. The flower's compact form and soft coloration make it a gentle accent in natural or landscaped urban environments.
description: A spherical cluster of white, narrow petals with green accents, supported by a slender, dark green stem. The flower has a layered, radial arrangement and a soft, delicate appearance.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from a slight angle, showing the dense, overlapping white petals and a hint of green at the base, with the stem curving gently.
description_view_1: This view presents the flower head more frontally, highlighting the symmetry of the petal arrangement and the straightness of the stem.
description_view_2: The flower is viewed from another side, revealing the consistent petal layering and a subtle green core, with the stem appearing slightly off-center.
description_view_3: A direct view of the flower head, emphasizing the radial symmetry and the even distribution of petals around the green center.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a dense, radial pattern forming a spherical cluster, with significant overlapping and layering, exhibiting radial symmetry typical of many wildflowers.
